title: Understanding the Rocket Equation
text: To find the initial mass of the rocket, we can use the rocket equation, which relates the change in velocity to the exhaust speed and the mass of the rocket.

title: Rocket Equation Formula
text: The rocket equation is given by: \( \Delta v $$=$$ $$v_e$$ \cdot \ln \left( \frac{m_i}{m_f} \right) \), where \( \Delta v \) is the change in velocity, \( $$v_e$$ \) is the exhaust speed, \( $$m_i$$ \) is the initial mass, and \( $$m_f$$ \) is the final mass.

title: Given Values
text: From the problem, we know: \( \Delta v $$=$$ $$100$$ \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$, \( $$v_e$$ $$=$$ $$1500$$ \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$, and the mass of fuel burned is $$100$$ kg.

title: Final Mass Calculation
text: The final mass \( $$m_f$$ \) is the initial mass \( $$m_i$$ \) minus the mass of the fuel burned. So, \( $$m_f$$ $$=$$ $$m_i$$ - $$100$$ \).

title: Solving for Initial Mass
text: Using the rocket equation \( \Delta v $$=$$ $$v_e$$ \cdot \ln \left( \frac{m_i}{m_f} \right) \), substitute the known values and solve for \( $$m_i$$ \).
